params.text: ## This is a demo demonstrating how you can use PyKEEN when you have a graph with numeric *node* attributes

We are going to use the same dataset as the MLP demo, so that we can compare results.

Save the edges as normal, but our features have to be in the following format: `node_id, feature_1, feature_2, ..., feature_n` with different features in different columns.
As normal we will need a training, testing and validation set, so we will split our full dataset with the ratio of `0.7` training, `0.2` testing and `0.1` validation.

## Model definition
To be able to use node attributes with PyKEEN models this special box needs to be used. The following parameters have to be given:
- Interaction: mostly the same as a given PyKEEN model, that model's scoring function will be used to score triples.
- Combination: There are a few options, here is what they do:
   - `ComplexSeparated`: Mainly for use with models that represent entities and relations with complex numbers. i.e. `ComplEx`
   - `ConcatProjection`: Combine representations by concatenation followed by a linear projection with configurable activation function and additional dropout capabilities.
   - `Gated`: Implements a gated linear transformation for the combination of entities and literals.
Compared to the other Combinations, this combination makes use of a gating mechanism commonly found in RNNs. The main goal of this gating mechanism is to learn which parts of the additional literal information is useful or not and act accordingly, by incorporating them into the new combined embedding or discarding them.

### Model evaluation
From the evaluation metrics gathered we can conclude the following:
- Accuracy is mostly the same whether we use attributes or not. But precision sees a big jump of ~15%.
- However `hits@k` will be much better (>10%) when using attributes.
- The model also converges faster
These are the metrics of the model that does not use node attributes.

## Model training

This step is the same as with traditional PyKEEN models. The rest of the pipeline is also functionally the same.

See more in the `Link prediction with PyKEEN` demo.
